aids.title: IC Number Memory
aids.content:
**7400 = NAND** (00 = N for NAND)
**7402 = NOR** (02 = O for nOR)
**7404 = NOT** (04 sounds like 'nOT')
**7408 = AND** (08 looks like 'AND')
**7432 = OR** (32 = 3+2 = OR has 2 inputs)
**7486 = XOR** (86 = X-86 like Intel)
aids.forRemembering: Common IC numbers for basic gates

aids.title: Gate Shape Memory
aids.content:
**AND** = D-shape (D for 'anD')
**OR** = Pointed arrow (points to output)
**NOT** = Triangle with bubble (inverts = circle)
**NAND/NOR** = Base gate + bubble
**XOR** = OR + extra curve (extra feature)

aids.title: NAND/NOR Universal Property
aids.content:
**NAND-y** or **NOR-mal** - both can do it ALL!

Universal means:
- Can make NOT (self-NAND or self-NOR)
- Can make AND (NAND + NOT)
- Can make OR (NOT inputs, then NAND)

Any gate can be built!
aids.forRemembering: Why NAND and NOR are universal

aids.title: Gate Analogy Story
aids.content:
Think of gates as **bouncers at a club**:

- **AND gate**: 'ALL of you must have ID' (all 1s needed)
- **OR gate**: 'ANY one of you with ID is enough' (one 1 is enough)
- **NOT gate**: 'Opposite day!' (flip the answer)
- **XOR gate**: 'Only one of you can enter' (different = enter)

aids.title: Seven Gates Rhyme
aids.content:
AND needs all, OR needs one,
NOT just flips when it is done.
NAND says no when AND says yes,
NOR negates OR, nothing less.
XOR checks if inputs disagree,
XNOR likes them same, you see!

aids.title: Bubble = NOT
aids.content:
Whenever you see a **bubble (small circle)** on a gate symbol, it means **inversion/NOT**.

- Bubble at output = output is inverted
- Bubble at input = that input is inverted

NAND = AND with bubble
NOR = OR with bubble
